Ad Text:
Over 30 years, we've grown from humble beginnings (founded 1995) to a global digital lottery technology leader (ASX: JIN), operating in Australia, the UK, Canada and the USA. We partner with thousands of good causes worldwide, helping raise over $290M in FY25 alone. Innovation is at the heart of everything we do.

What will you be doing?

This role sits within the Define pipeline, where work is shaped before it reaches engineering. In this newly created role as a Delivery Lead, you will be responsible for turning prioritised business initiatives into a consistent flow of build-ready work, partnering closely with Business Analysts and Product Designers to move ideas from early input through to clear, actionable specifications.

Working as the lead for this function, you will help establish and refine ways of working across the business. This is a strong fit for someone who enjoys roadmap planning, program-style delivery, backlog health, and stakeholder communication, and who is motivated by improving how work flows through a system.

While this role is not accountable for engineering delivery itself, it plays a critical role in defining what is being worked on and when it is ready to flow into delivery teams.

Reports to: Principal Engineering Manager

What Success Looks Like

Consistent flow of high-quality, build-ready work into engineering

Clear visibility of pipeline status, timelines, risks, and dependencies

Reduced ambiguity and improved delivery predictability

Strong stakeholder trust and alignment

A healthy buffer of shaped work to support engineering continuity

Key Challenges

Building a new function from the ground up

Managing stakeholder expectations during early-stage maturity

Balancing structure, governance, and speed

Operating effectively in a dual structure (not owning engineering delivery directly)

What Your Day-to-Day Will Look Like

Lead the flow of work through the Define pipeline, guiding initiatives from intake through to Definition of Ready.

Work with Business Analysts and Product Designers as a combined working group to shape requirements and supporting artefacts.

Manage and prioritise backlog flow to ensure engineering teams have a consistent pipeline of well-defined work

Coordinate planning and scheduling across incoming initiatives based on capacity and business priorities

Act as the key communication point for status updates, risks, and delays relating to defined work

Provide clear reporting and progress updates to senior stakeholders and the steering group responsible for prioritised initiatives.

Support agile ways of working within the team, using practices aligned to Scrum, Kanban, or a similar framework.s

Establish structure, cadence, and repeatable processes for the function as it matures

Produce and maintain high-quality planning artefacts, including roadmaps, backlog views, and status documentation.

Escalate pipeline risks where there is insufficient shaped work to support downstream engineering demand.

What You’ll Need to Bring to the Role

Demonstrated experience in business analysis, delivery leadership, program coordination, or backlog management within a technology environment

Tertiary qualification in Business Analysis, Project Management, Information Technology, or a related discipline, or equivalent relevant experience

Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to communicate clearly with senior business stakeholders

Strong facilitation skills, with experience running workshops and stakeholder discussions to elicit and validate requirements

A solid understanding of agile principles and experience working within software delivery environments

Experience with structured requirements practices, including MoSCoW prioritisation, user story writing, and Definition of Ready / Definition of Done frameworks

Ability to manage work through structured workflows and bring clarity to evolving or loosely defined inputs

Proficiency in planning tools such as roadmaps, Gantt charts, and backlog management systems

Demonstrated leadership experience leading and developing small, cross-functional teams

Experience creating and presenting clear planning artefacts, delivery plans, and status updates to stakeholders

Nice to Have

Exposure to frameworks or certifications such as SAFe, Scrum Master, or Kanban

Experience working on SaaS or digital product environments

Salary Banding: $150,000 - $170,000 + Super

Our Ways of Working

At Jumbo, we value balance and flexibility. Based in Brisbane and working out of our Head Office, our hybrid working model combines in-office collaboration (three days per week) with WFH work (two days per week). We also offer flexible working hours, with core collaboration times from 10 AM to 2 PM.

Why Join Jumbo?

Find Your Freedom - https://bit.ly/Jumbointeractive

At Jumbo, our team thrives because we invest in our people. Here’s what you can expect from us;

A short-term incentive program that rewards your contributions, dependent on the company's performance

Access to our Employee Assistance Program, providing counselling and coaching

Award-winning talent management programs for career growth and skill-building

On-site chefs (Brisbane) offering fresh, gourmet lunches daily and breakfast twice a week

The freedom to work from almost anywhere in the world for up to 120 days a year

A social culture, with activities like lunchtime chess, Jumbo band, corporate games, an indoor rock climbing group, Friday socials, and events celebrating diversity and inclusion

Generous parental leave: 14 weeks of paid leave for primary carers and 4 weeks for partners

Candidates who are offered a position will be required to complete pre-employment checks through our partner, Checkmate.
We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Gold Employer – we commit to responding to every applicant.

Ad Text:
Newcastle Greater Mutual Group Ltd (NGM Group) offers retail banking services to more than half a million Australians under the brands Greater Bank and Newcastle Permanent.  As one of Australia’s largest customer-owned banks, we’re a driving force in customer owned banking, operating under the trusted brands Greater Bank and Newcastle Permanent. With over 200 years of combined experience, we’ve helped hundreds of thousands of Australians achieve home ownership and providing a genuine alternative to the big banks—all while strengthening the communities we serve.

NGM Group is searching for a Senior Risk Specialist. The position is full time and based in Newcastle, NSW.

Tasks and Responsibilities

Assess and mitigate risks related to emerging technologies, data initiatives, and cyber security, including cloud encryption, identity and access management (IAM), and other critical technology controls.

Provide independent oversight and challenge for major change initiatives, including technology, data, cyber, and operational projects.

Design and implement risk assurance strategies and frameworks for change programs, embedding compliance-by-design and risk appetite considerations.

Develop and maintain change-portfolio assurance plans in collaboration with Line 1 teams and Internal Audit, ensuring coverage is comprehensive and non-duplicative.

Support integration and transformation programs by embedding risk controls throughout all phases: discovery, design, execution, transition, and postimplementation.

Collaborate with program teams, delivery teams, and business SMEs to identify, evaluate, and manage operational, regulatory, and compliance risks in change initiatives.

Ensure alignment with NGM Group’s risk appetite, privacy frameworks, data protection requirements (e.g., GDPR), cloud security standards, and regulatory expectations.

Influence and advise program sponsors and senior leadership on risk decisions, including recommendations for enhanced controls and mitigation strategies.

Monitor and report on change-related risks to senior management and stakeholders, supporting informed decision-making and maintaining a strong risk-aware culture.

Contribute to the continuous improvement of change risk management practices, including lessons learned, risk frameworks, and assurance methodologies.




What are we looking for?

Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Commerce

At least 15+ years' experience in Risk, Audit/Assurance or Compliance related roles

Experience in the financial services industry (preferably retail banking) and driving change in a technology driven environment

Experience managing multiple stakeholders, projects or initiatives

Sound knowledge of operational risk as well as other material risk types

Sound knowledge of relevant regulatory and compliance obligation

Experience in governance and control frameworks within ADIs

Deep understanding of risk management frameworks, second-line oversight functions, and the three-lines-of-defence model.

Knowledge of banking regulatory requirements related to change and operational risk (e.g., APRA CPS 231, BCBS, ASIC guidance).

Experience in risk assessment, change governance, internal control monitoring, and remediation tracking.

Familiarity with project and program management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall) as applied to change initiatives.

Ability to identify operational, compliance, and technology risks in complex business transformations.

Competence in reporting risk insights clearly and concisely to senior stakeholders.




What can you expect from us?

NGM Group employees can take advantage of the following work perks:

Health and wellbeing; Fitness passport, corporate health insurance and annual flu vaccination.

Lifestyle and giving back; Hotel discounts, 18 weeks paid parental leave, two community volunteers days and three recreational leave days per year.

Professional advancement; Flexible work arrangements, recognition programs and employee referral program.

 

NGM Group is committed to delivering trusted banking to enable our customers and communities to thrive. We do this by leading with heart, doing the right things in the right way, and by maintaining high standards of ethical behaviour in everything that we do. All applicants are therefore required to undergo a comprehensive background check, Bankruptcy Check and a National Police Check.

We are also working to reflect the vibrant communities we serve. We believe in creating an inclusive, diverse, and supportive workplace where everyone can thrive. If you require adjustments to be made during the recruitment process or would like to discuss any accessibility requirements, your Talent Acquisition Partner will be happy to engage in a confidential discussion and assist. Your unique talents and perspectives are what make us stronger.

Location: Macquarie Park, NSW or Malvern East, VIC or Ormeau, QLD (Hybrid)



Drive smarter workforce decisions. Improve performance. Support exceptional care.

At VetPartners, we support more than 270 veterinary clinics across Australia and New Zealand, enabling our teams to deliver outstanding care to pets and their owners.

We are seeking a Workforce Planning Optimisation Manager to play a critical role in strengthening how labour is forecast, managed, and optimised to perform at its best across our network. This is a high-impact position working closely with senior operational leaders to drive smarter workforce decisions, improve efficiency, and support sustainable clinic performance.




About the Role

Reporting to the General Manager Business Integration, you will sit at the intersection of operations, data, and workforce strategy. You will partner with General Managers, Regional Managers, and clinic leaders to embed consistent workforce planning practices, improve system capability, and provide meaningful insights that drive action.

From forecasting demand and modelling scenarios to improving rostering practices and reducing labour inefficiencies, you will directly influence how our clinics operate day to day.

Ultimately, your work will ensure the right people are in the right place at the right time, supporting better patient care, stronger team experiences, and improved business outcomes.




Key Responsibilities

Workforce Analytics & Forecasting

Analyse clinic performance data to identify trends and cost opportunities

Forecast workforce demand based on seasonality, growth, and new services

Build scenario models to support strategic workforce decisions

Labour Cost Optimisation

Monitor labour-to-revenue ratios and performance benchmarks

Identify and reduce overtime and locum reliance

Support incentive and bonus structures aligned to productivity

Workforce Reporting & Governance

Deliver weekly, monthly, and ad hoc workforce insights to senior leaders

Develop dashboards tracking key KPIs (utilisation, vacancies, compliance)

Drive consistency in rostering, timesheets, and workforce processes

Operational & Strategic Initiatives

Lead key workforce projects

Support rollout and adoption of optimised base rosters

Partner with teams across operations, finance, and data to deliver improvements

Scheduling & Capability Building

Standardise rostering practices across clinics

Develop tools, guides, and playbooks to support teams

Build workforce planning capability at clinic and regional level




About You

You are a data-driven operator and strategic thinker who thrives in fast-paced, multi-site environments. You’re comfortable working with senior stakeholders, influencing decisions through insight and leveraging your broad experience.

You will bring:

Experience in workforce planning, analytics, or operational optimisation

Strong ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights

Experience with forecasting, scenario modelling, and workforce metrics

Proven ability to influence and drive change across diverse teams

Understanding of labour cost drivers and operational KPIs

Experience with workforce systems (e.g. Humanforce, Emplive) highly regarded




Why join VetPartners

Purpose-led work: Make a meaningful difference every day by supporting those who care for pets and their families

Autonomy & impact: Own an end-to-end L&D function with real influence

Collaborative culture: Work alongside passionate, committed people who value learning and growth

Development opportunities: Ongoing professional development and the chance to grow with a dynamic organisation

 

Apply Now

Job Title: Technology Business Analyst (12mth Fixed Term Contract) - New role
Ad Text:
Additional BA role joining our highly regarded Technology team, leading on transformational projects
As the conduit between business and technology delivery teams, your points of difference will be your critical thinking; technical acumen; exceptional communication skills; and an enjoyment in being able to support the broader Technology team in delivering fit for purpose technology solutions
If you thrive in a high performance, high expectation environment, underpinned by a collaborative culture, keep reading.

Who are we?

We are Mallesons. The top-tier independent firm from Australia, a full-service firm trusted on the most complex and consequential matters across our region and around the world. We've been part of Australia's story for almost 200 years and acknowledge Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the traditional owners and custodians of these lands and waters.

Our independence gives us the flexibility to combine our extensive top tier full-service capability with the best advisers in every market. With deep expertise, collective judgment and genuine collaboration, we cut through complexity so our clients can move forward with confidence and make more happen.

Role Detail

Joining the Technology Projects team, part of the wider Technology team of approximately 100 nationally, you will collaborate with stakeholders to understand their needs, translate them into clear and actionable requirements, capture 'as is' and model 'to be' business processes and workflows, identifying opportunities for process improvements and to ensure solutions meet project outcomes and deliver business value.

Reporting to the Technology Business Analyst Lead, with flexibility to be Melbourne or Sydney CBD based, key responsibilities include:

Working with partners and staff to capture end to end business requirements for systems and processes in line with the Project Management framework. Requirements will include 'as-is' and 'to-be' process diagrams.
Identifying issues, risks and benefits of existing and proposed solutions and identifying opportunities
for process improvements.
Capturing current state metrics (resourcing, time and volume) and highlighting pain points and
optimisation opportunities.
Assisting with the development of project concept documentation and business case documentation,
Making use of the insight and knowledge gained through your BA engagement.
Working alongside those responsible for implementing projects to ensure that the project outcomes are consistent with the business requirements and expectations.
Maintaining the documented business requirements for solutions throughout the design and development process, handing over to the business owner once the solution is in production.
Working with systems to drive efficiency and use process reengineering to improve and upgrade systems.

About You

You are someone who can anticipate problems before they happen; bridge gaps seamlessly; and have an ability to focus on value delivery, not just requirement completion.

To become a trusted advisor, not just an intermediary, you will be a pragmatic problem-solver with a keen eye for detail, and an ability to align technical solutions with the needs of the business to drive efficiencies and improve business performance.

As a clear communicator with an ability to make the complex simple, translating the non-technical into technical, and vice versa, you will liaise with various stakeholders, across various levels, needing a confidence that comes from experience. You will also bring:

Solid BA or technical analyst experience in legal and/or professional services
Previous experience working on data related initiatives and programs, to hit the ground running and lead the analysis and discovery stages of data projects in the pipeline (projects not limited to data)
Process mapping tool experience (eg flowcharting software or Unified Modelling Language)
Advanced Microsoft Office skills, i.e Word, Excel, Visio and PowerPoint
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Ability to engage and build professional relationships with people at all levels of the organisation
Ability to work on multiple projects with competing priorities effectively
A natural curiosity

Next Steps

Please click 'Apply Now' to apply online with your cover letter summarising why you're applying and your resume to support your experience.

Applications close 5pm Monday 20th April 2026.

If you have any questions or need any further information, please don't hesitate to reach out to Bridget on 02) 9296 3563.

If you are not an Australian citizen or permanent resident, you must hold all visas and other approvals necessary for you to work for the firm in Australia.

Why work with us?

We build careers that go everywhere. Mallesons is a special place to work where anything is possible, ambitions are limitless and diversity and inclusion play an important role. It's a place where you will collaborate with exceptional colleagues and clients, where you will sharpen your edge and support others to do the same, where you will make a difference you can be proud of. You will have access to a suite of wellbeing initiatives, benefits, policies and process that will support you to achieve your career goals.

Together we learn. Together we master. Together we lead.

Diversity and Inclusion

Mallesons is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and people from diverse backgrounds. We welcome, value, support and harness the positive benefits that people's different life experiences bring, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages.

If you are unsure if you meet all criteria for this role, but have a passion for delivering high quality work, developing your skills and realising your full potential, then we encourage you to apply and have a further conversation about how you could fit at Mallesons.

We have a range of inclusive hiring practices - if you would like to seek workplace adjustments to complete your application please contact the Recruitment contact for this role or email careers@mallesons.com

🟠 Data Lead

📍 Melbourne (Hybrid)

💰 $160–190k + Super




Fix the data. Change the business.




We’re working with a growing Australian retail and distribution business that’s investing heavily in its systems, data and operations.

They’re not hiring someone to build dashboards.




They’re hiring someone to make sense of a complex, slightly chaotic data landscape, and turn it into something the business actually trusts and uses.

New systems. Expanding footprint. Plenty of moving parts.




Right now?

The data’s there… but it’s not working hard enough.

That’s where you come in.




🧠 What you’ll actually be doing




You’ll sit at the centre of the business and:

Build Power BI dashboards that people actually use (not just look at once and forget)
Pull together data from across ERP, POS, eCom, CRM and more — and make it make sense
Get stuck into messy, fragmented data and clean it up properly
Own the SQL + ETL layer (Azure environment)
Automate manual processes where you see them




Work directly with stakeholders across:

Sales
Supply Chain
Finance
Product / Procurement
Marketing




This is end-to-end ownership — not just reporting, not just engineering.




🧩 What we’re looking for




You’ve probably been:

A Senior Data Analyst / BI Analyst / Analytics Engineer
Sitting in a retail, distribution, or multi-site business
Close to the commercial side — not buried in a silo




And you:

Are strong in Power BI (DAX, modelling, storytelling)
Can write proper SQL (not just SELECT *)
Have worked with ETL pipelines + Azure (or similar)
Aren’t scared of dirty data — you actually enjoy fixing it
Can talk to non-technical people without making their eyes glaze over




Bonus points if you’ve used Python or started playing with AI tooling — but this isn’t a “buzzword bingo” role.




🚫 What this role is NOT




Let’s be clear:

Not a “Head of Data” strategy role
Not a pure Data Engineer role
Not a dashboard factory with zero ownership




This is for someone who wants to:

👉 roll their sleeves up

👉 own the problem

👉 build something that actually matters




🌱 Why it’s interesting

You’ll have real impact across the business from day one
Flat structure — access to decision makers
Hybrid working with genuine flexibility
A business that actually wants to use data better




And importantly…

👉 You won’t inherit something perfect

👉 You’ll build it properly

💬 The vibe




Straight-talking. Low ego. Get stuff done.




If you like:

Over-engineering
Endless meetings
Hiding behind jargon

…this won’t be your thing.




If you like:

Solving real problems
Working with good people
Seeing your work actually used




You’ll feel right at home.




📩 Interested?




Drop me a message or apply

At Santos, we do work that matters and always have. When you join us, so will you.

The difference isn’t just thinking big – it’s delivering on it too.

What you’ll do:

The role also provides technical support and continuous improvement of systems and processes, including Microsoft Dynamics 365, to enhance efficiency, data integrity, and compliance outcomes.

Draft and manage land access contracts and related documentation 
Perform quality assurance to ensure accuracy and compliance of contracts 
Ensure adherence to compensation terms across QLD, NSW, SA, and NT landholder agreements
Analyse and review annual landholder compensation arrangements 
Design and maintain end-to-end business processes within Microsoft Dynamics 365 
Ensure systems are configured for best practice, scalability, and security 
Develop and run test cases to validate system functionality and workflows 
Create functional documentation, process maps, and user training materials 
Identify and implement process improvements, automation, and system enhancements 
Provide ongoing system support, maintain data quality, and assist stakeholders across departments
  
What you’ll bring: 
We are looking for a motivated and experienced candidate with a strong analytical and problem-solving mindset, complemented by excellent communication and interpersonal skills. You bring a proactive approach to your work, with the ability to take ownership, manage competing priorities, and deliver high-quality outcomes. Your attention to detail, collaborative style, and drive for continuous improvement will enable you to build strong relationships and contribute to positive business outcomes. You will also be able to demonstrate the following:

Strong contract and economic analysis capability 
Proven experience translating business requirements into meaningful data insights and reporting solutions 
Experience in data modelling and structuring datasets for reporting and analytics 
Proficiency in Microsoft Dynamics 365, Power BI, and advanced Microsoft Excel

Data Governance Manager

Fulltime Permanent

Hybrid, Darlinghurst, Sydney, NSW

Reporting to: IT Director

About you


You are a strategic and collaborative data governance professional who thrives in complex, research-driven environments. You bring a strong balance of governance rigour and practical problem solving, with the ability to translate policy into meaningful, usable frameworks for researchers and technical teams alike. Passionate about enabling high quality, ethical and secure use of data, you enjoy working at the intersection of science, technology and compliance. Above all, you value partnership, clarity and purpose in the work you do.

The role

Reporting to the IT Director, the Data Governance Manager will lead the development and implementation of the Institute's organisation wide data governance strategy. This is a newly established role, created in response to the rapid growth and scale of our research data environment, which currently manages approximately 1.5 petabytes of diverse and complex data assets.

In this pivotal position, you will establish clear accountability for data stewardship, design scalable governance frameworks, and ensure compliance with legal, ethical and funding obligations. You will work closely with researchers, IT teams and executive leaders to enable secure, efficient and transparent data sharing; supporting data driven discovery while reducing administrative burden across the Institute. A Data Governance Officer role will be introduced in the future and will report to you once established.

What we will trust you with  
Leading the development and execution of the Institute's data governance strategy, aligned with research objectives and regulatory requirements
Designing and implementing institute-wide data governance frameworks, policies and standards that support current and future data growth
Establishing and chairing cross-functional data governance committees to drive accountability and consistency
Defining data ownership, stewardship roles and escalation pathways across research divisions and operational teams
Developing and enforcing data quality standards, metadata frameworks and data catalogues to improve data discoverability and reproducibility
Overseeing data security, access controls, classification schemes and incident response processes
Enabling secure internal and external data sharing through robust governance, agreements and collaboration frameworks
Driving stakeholder engagement, training and cultural change to embed data governance best practice across the Institute
Does this sound like you?
You hold an advanced degree in data science, information management, computer science or a related field
At least five years' experience in data governance, data management or information governance roles
You have successfully led the design and implementation of data governance frameworks in complex organisations
Strong knowledge of data governance best practice and frameworks such as DAMA-DMBOK or DCAM
Solid understanding of Australian and international privacy legislation and research ethics requirements
Experienced in data quality, metadata management and data cataloguing approaches
Confident working with highly diverse stakeholders, from researchers to executive leaders
Able to communicate clearly and effectively, with the ability to influence, educate and build trust

About us

The Victor Chang Cardiac Research Institute (the Institute) is dedicated to finding cures for cardiovascular disease and is one of the most respected heart research facilities in the world. Renowned for the quality of its breakthroughs, the Institute was established in 1994 and has grown to a team of more than 200 researchers from around the globe, working across five divisions.

A global centre-of-excellence, it integrates Cardiac Physiology and Transplantation, Developmental and Stem Cell Biology, Molecular Cardiology and Biophysics, Structural and Computational Biology, as well as Vascular Biology. At the Institute, we translate our discoveries into new diagnostics, prevention, and treatment of cardiovascular disease - reducing the incidence, severity, and impact of heart disease. ​


What you will be proud of

Being part of an Institute that is not only dedicated to finding cures for cardiovascular disease, but one that also strives to ensure our employees and students enjoy a great working environment. We value and are committed to providing a safe working environment that embraces diversity, gender equity and promote flexible working arrangements for all employees.

Qualifications and Experience

Technical Skills: Advanced technical proficiency in Excel and the ability to learn and optimise HRIS remuneration modules.

About the Role

This is a 2 year Max Term Contract

As a Senior Analyst – Quantitative Modelling, you will play a key role in supporting credit risk model development, monitoring, and the migration of modelling infrastructure into modern cloud-based environments. You will be responsible for leading the development and ongoing performance management of a range of credit risk models, including Probability of Default (PD), Loss Given Default (LGD), Exposure at Default (EAD), scorecards, and rating models, ensuring they are robust, fit-for-purpose, and analytically sound.

You will ensure that all models meet regulatory and internal governance requirements, including compliance with frameworks such as IFRS 9, Basel, and APRA standards. This role requires strong judgement and attention to detail, as you will contribute to regulatory submissions and actively engage in audit and assurance reviews, clearly articulating model methodologies, assumptions, and outcomes to internal and external stakeholders.

Collaboration is central to this role. You will work closely with stakeholders across risk, finance, and business units to effectively embed credit risk models into decision-making processes. Your ability to translate complex quantitative concepts into practical insights will be critical to enabling informed business outcomes.

In addition, you will build and maintain automated tools and dashboards to monitor model performance and support reporting within a cloud environment. You will also provide mentorship to junior team members, fostering capability uplift and knowledge sharing across the team while contributing to a strong and collaborative modelling culture.

About You

You bring a strong understanding of credit risk modelling techniques and the regulatory frameworks that underpin them, including IFRS 9, Basel, and APRA requirements. You have hands-on experience working with quantitative models and are comfortable using programming languages such as Python, R, SAS, and SQL to analyse data, develop models, and generate insights. You also hold a bachelor or postgraduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Econometrics, Actuarial Science, or Engineering.

You are an effective communicator who can translate complex technical concepts into clear and compelling insights for both technical and non-technical audiences. You have intermediate to advanced data visualisation and storytelling skills, using tools such as R, Python, or Power BI to communicate model performance, drivers, and implications in a meaningful way.

You are passionate about mentoring others and driving innovation in risk analytics. You are enthusiastic about modernising modelling workflows by building and migrating tools and processes to scalable, cloud-based analytics platforms such as Databricks. Experience with Azure DevOps and Databricks is highly regarded, along with a collaborative mindset and a desire to continuously improve how risk models are developed, governed, and embedded in decision-making.

Job Title: Program Manager – Growth and Operational Excellence
Ad Text:
Job Title: Program Manager – Growth & Operational Excellence

Location: Role can be based in Sydney (St Leonards), Melbourne (St Kilda) or Brisbane, but a flexible approach to WFH is supported. 

Job Type: Permanent Part-Time (80%)

 

Job Summary:

Reporting to the Director, Growth & Operational Excellence, this pivotal role offers an exceptional opportunity for a Program Manager to serve as a key strategic partner within our Growth & Operational Excellence team.

You will support the delivery of high-impact, data-driven performance improvement initiatives, ensuring initiatives align seamlessly with our strategic objectives, resolving roadblocks, and keeping the team on track.

This role requires a proactive leader who can anticipate challenges, facilitate executive-level collaboration, and translate strategic vision into actionable programs.

Key Responsibilities:

Program Leadership: Coordinate and facilitate the design and implementation of multiple programs of work, ensuring direct alignment with our strategic objectives and delivering tangible, high-impact outcomes across the organisation. This includes planning and leading detailed quarterly planning sessions, preparing for Quarterly Business Reviews, and ensuring structures and ways of working are set up to deliver on planned outcomes.

Strategic Reporting & Insights: Design and prepare executive-level presentations covering program progress, outcomes, and decisions required to senior executive management, providing data-driven recommendations that inform strategic decision-making.

Strategic Communication & Stakeholder Engagement: Cultivate clear, concise, and impactful communication across all levels, from team members to site executive leadership to the Australian senior executive team. You will drive consensus and foster productive relationships, acting as a critical interface, translating executive priorities into a clear program plan and facilitating seamless cross-functional collaboration.

Proactive Risk Mitigation: Anticipate potential program obstacles and proactively develop robust contingency plans, ensuring the smooth progression and successful delivery of initiatives.

Performance Insight & Optimisation: Maintain and improve on existing frameworks to monitor program effectiveness and delivery. You will proactively identify areas for improvement, guiding necessary adjustments and resolutions.

Budget & Resource Stewardship: Develop and manage program budgets, including forecasting, tracking expenses, and strategically reallocating resources to maximise program effectiveness and efficiency.

Qualifications:

Education: A Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Management, Business Administration, or a related field is essential.

Experience: A minimum of 5 years of demonstrated experience in program management, strategic consulting, or a related role, with a proven track record of delivering complex, high-impact projects. Experience related to the health sector is preferred.

Skills: Exceptional leadership, strategic communication, and organisational skills. Advanced proficiency in data analysis, business intelligence tools, and project/program management software. Ability to influence without direct authority.

Personal Attributes:

Strategic & Results-Oriented: Possesses a keen strategic mindset with an unwavering focus on delivering high-quality, measurable outcomes that advance organisational goals.

Adaptable & Resilient: Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, demonstrating exceptional adaptability and resilience when navigating complex challenges.

Proactive Problem Solver: A proactive approach to identifying challenges and an innate ability to develop practical, innovative solutions.

Trusted Advisor: Builds strong relationships based on trust and credibility, acting as a reliable advisor to senior leadership and cross-functional teams.

High Integrity & Professionalism: Upholds the highest standards of integrity, professionalism, and ethical conduct.

 

About the Growth & Operational Excellence Team

Ramsay’s Growth & Operational Excellence team has built a strong reputation across the business for identifying and implementing practical, high-impact, and data-driven performance improvements through close collaboration with Operations Executive Teams. We combine an extensive range of skills and experience from both within and outside the business, and we pride ourselves on being dynamic, constructive, and collaborative.

This opportunity is ideal for a motivated and experienced Program Manager ready to help address the most pressing operational challenges and opportunities in the business. We are seeking someone who is passionate about using their strategic problem-solving acumen, analytical mindset, and strong relationship-building toolkit to support our hospitals to deliver high-value healthcare.

Benefits

Competitive salary and an extensive benefits package that recognise and reward your valuable contributions while supporting your overall well-being.

Enhance your career trajectory through a wealth of professional development initiatives and clearly defined pathways for advancement, empowering you to realise your full potential within our organisation.

Enjoy access to a premier rewards platform that offers exceptional discounts and privileges from renowned retailers, enriching your lifestyle both personally and professionally.

Embrace the opportunity to drive transformative change in the delivery of high-quality healthcare services, making a significant contribution to the evolution of our industry for the better.

 

About Us

Ramsay Health Care, a global operator of private hospitals and primary care clinics with over 70 Australian sites employing over 34,000 people. We have been focused on delivering high-quality patient care and practising the Ramsay Way philosophy of ‘People Caring for People’ since 1964.

We believe in a bright future for our company and for every one of our employees – our wellbeing initiatives and learning and development programs have seen us grow as an industry leader and sought after employer.

Requirements

A National Police Check conducted within the previous 12 months may be asked of you

To Apply

Please apply via the email provided: FerrarisE@ramsayhealth.com.au

Applications made by recruitment agencies will not be considered.

Applications close: 23 April 2026

Ramsay Health Care is committed to Child Safety.  Details of our commitment are set out in our Code of Conduct, available at www.ramsayhealth.com

Job Title: Program Risk Analyst and Reporting Coordinator
Ad Text:
Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:

Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown economies, improved the resiliency of the world's infrastructure, increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the world a safer, cleaner place. 

Differentiated by the quality of our people and our relentless drive to deliver the most successful outcomes, we align our capabilities to our customers' objectives to create a lasting positive impact. We serve the Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security & Environmental; Energy; Mining & Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span from initial planning and investment, through start-up and operations. 

Core to Bechtel is our Vision, Values and Commitments. They are what we believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver.

Project Overview:

Bechtel helps clients deliver projects of purpose that create a lasting positive legacy globally. These large, complex and multi-disciplined projects create jobs and grow economies; improve the resiliency of the world’s infrastructure; connect communities to resources and opportunities; get us closer to net zero; tackle critical environmental challenges to protect people and the planet; and accelerate progress to make the world a cleaner, greener, safer place.  Bechtel is providing a wide variety project and contract management services, crossing all disciplines, to customers in Western Australia to support exciting capital works programmes, including in aviation, defence and energy infrastructure. We are looking for people to join us in a long-term relationship to deliver these services in and around Perth where we are working within integrated teams with our customers and to help build Bechtel’s capabilities for even greater opportunities in the future

Job Summary:

As the Program Risk Analyst and Reporting Coordinator you will be responsible for supporting Project Risk Management (PRM) and relevant internal and external stakeholders in PRM matters across the assigned program or project. The role requires a people person with ability to drive focus on PRM, influence without authority and tie PRM with key interfaces across the assigned scope.

Major Responsibilities:

Supports implementation of the project risk management program across the assigned program or project. 

Responsible for the development, deployment, and management of PRM data collection, dashboards, analysis tools and reports.

Maintain risk and control registers and assist with risk reporting across the program.

Responsible for quantitative risk analysis (schedule and cost) for assigned program.

Supports the enterprise analysis of project risks.

Support and maintain risk activities to ensure alignment with the risk management plan.

Supports, coordinates and runs risk workshops on projects.

Coordinating and consolidating input for regular risk reports and verifies consistency with previous reports to ensure compatibility and consistency

Supports preparation of board papers as required

Coordinates risk reporting practices for compliance with applicable standards

Education and Experience Requirements:

Typically requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 5-8 years of relevant experience or 9-12 years of relevant work experience.

Skill in working and communicating effectively, both orally and in writing, with other project functional groups. Proven ability to build strong relationships with customers and team members.

Demonstrated experience in qualitative and quantitative risk management is necessary. 

Experience in the infrastructure / heavy civil / aviation / renewables / construction industries advantageous  

High attention to detail and accuracy in managing documents and data.

Total Rewards/Benefits:

For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world's toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people thrive.  Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total Rewards

Diverse teams build the extraordinary:

As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This diversity has made us a more trusted partner, more effective problem solvers and innovators, and a more attractive destination for leading talent.

We are committed to being a company where every colleague feels that they belong-where colleagues feel part of "One Team," respected and rewarded for what they bring, supported in pursuing their goals, invested in our values and purpose, and treated equitably.

Job Title: Senior Sales Performance Specialist (12 Month Contract)
Ad Text:
Changing Lives, Transforming Careers

At Amplifon, we’re more than a company. As the Global Leader in hearing care, we combine cutting-edge solutions with a personal touch to create unforgettable experiences for our customers.

With a shared vision to empower people and push boundaries, we thrive in a culture built on care, collaboration, and impact. Here, your work fuels innovation, growth, and a purpose that resonates far beyond the ordinary.

Here, you’ll find all the support, tools, and opportunities you need to grow, whether it’s through our top-class development programs or by taking on projects in other parts of the world. And your colleagues will be there to motivate and inspire you every step of the way.

Position Purpose:

We are currently looking for a skilled and ambitious Sales Performance Manager to join our Retail Excellence team. 
The Retail Excellence department aims to provide full control of the most relevant business and in store KPIs to drive sales and productivity, hitting company targets and guaranteeing continuous funnel improvement through the best in-store customer experience. 
The main responsibility is to lead the Area Managers in all data visualization and BI activities as well as providing detailed analysis of the sales KPIs to for shop performance improvement. 
The Sales Performance Manager will be partnering with key stakeholders within the Company, will be part of a dynamic and young team, motivated to do the extra mile every day. He/she will work on both medium- and long-term projects in addition to contingency activities.

Key Accountabilities and Responsibilities: 

Monitor average length of each funnel segment, setting targets, verifying achievement, and defining action plans if required. 
Work along with IT, Supply Chain, and other company functions to reduce unproductive time, boosting productivity of our retail structure. 
Ensure Operative Protocols and appropriate execution. 
Regularly share KPIs and best practices among Amplifon Countries with a focus of KPIs and routines of our Retail structure. 
Guarantee the best in store customer experience, working alongside the marketing department and acting as the connection point between Marketing and the Field Organization. 
Lead the design, launch and implementation of all projects related to in-store excellence.

Experience and Qualifications: 

Essential Qualifications

Master or bachelor’s degree in Economics, Engineering or Statistics.

Essential Experience 

Expertise in management of complex structures of data analysis, aligning to business impacts.
Excellent knowledge of Office package, with advanced expertise in Excel (pivot, statistics formulas) and PowerPoint to assemble and present information.
Knowledge of Power B.I., in addition SQL knowledge will be considered as a plus. 
Experience in industries and Projects where performance maximization is key.

Desired Experience:

Prior experience working in a global or international organisation. 
Previous experience in Retail is desirable.

Skills and Competencies: 

Soft Skills

Alignment to the Amplifon Values .
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to manage and prioritise competing demands with a customer-focused and outcomes-cantered approach.
Adaptable to a dynamic environment with rapidly changing priorities.

Technical Skills

Strong analytical capability.
Results oriented – commitment to targets. 
Presentation skills - ability to translate complex analysis into key simple insights.
Business-oriented strategic vision.
Stakeholder management (local & global)

Putting People First

One of the ways we amplify careers is by helping our people achieve their full potential. We do so by providing constant constructive feedback and training opportunities that empower our people to excel. 

In addition, if you’re ready to put in the hard work, we’ll make sure your dedication and achievements are recognised.

Ready to take the next step in your career? Amplify your purpose with Amplifon!

As an employer that embraces Equal Opportunity and promotes inclusion and diversity, we encourage people of all ages and backgrounds to apply.

About Orica

At Orica, it’s the power of our people that leads change and shapes our futures. Every day, all around the world, our people help mobilise vital resources essential to progress. Established in 1874, we have grown to become the world leader in mining and civil blasting with a diverse of team of more than 13,000 across the world. 

It’s an exciting time to join us – we are shaping the future of mining through digital and automated technologies, embracing new ways of thinking, pioneering innovation and reimagining the way we work.

About the role

We are currently searching for a commercially minded process leader to join Orica as Senior Manager – Asset Management (GPO). Partnering closely with senior leaders across a diverse operational portfolio, you will strengthen how we govern and improve Asset Management end-to-end. You will join a high-performing, geographically diverse global process team and report to the Vice President – Global Process, Strategy and Enablement.

In this enterprise-facing role, you’ll be the central point for Asset Management process and system change. You’ll help teams define what’s needed, shape demand into clear options, and lead value-based prioritisation through established governance forums. Working closely with Functional Excellence, IT and regional teams, you’ll maintain global process standards, coordinate business readiness for change, and use performance insights to lift outcomes.

Success in this role requires sound judgement, credibility with senior leaders, and the ability to navigate complexity while staying focused on long-term organisational health and performance.

The role can be based in Melbourne, Brisbane or Newcastle, Perth and operates on a Monday–Friday hybrid arrangement (four days in the office, one day working from home).

Applications close Tuesday, 14 April.

What you will be doing

Own the end-to-end Asset Management process strategy and set the global direction

Establish and maintain the global process baseline, including standards, governance and decision rights

Act as the front door for process and system demand—qualify requests, clarify the problem, shape options, and ensure the right work enters the pipeline.

Lead cross-functional prioritisation using agreed, value-based frameworks and decision forums

Coordinate business readiness for strategic change, including UAT planning/coordination, communications, training and adoption support

Ensure change is planned, funded and resourced appropriately, with clear ownership across stakeholders

Monitor process performance using insights and KPIs, identify issues early, and drive continuous improvement

Track benefits against approved business cases, report progress clearly, and drive corrective action where outcomes are off track

What you will bring

Demonstrated experience leading global or multi-region process ownership, governance or enterprise process improvement (Asset Management exposure preferred)

Proven ability to shape demand and translate ambiguous requests into clear problem statements, options and recommended paths forward

Experience coordinating business readiness activities across complex stakeholder groups, including UAT, change, communications and training.

Comfortable influencing senior stakeholders and aligning cross-functional teams.

Analytical mindset with experience defining measures, using insights, and tracking benefits to ensure outcomes are realised.

Clear, practical communication style and the ability to bring structure to complex problems.

What we offer

As part of a truly global company, you will have the ability to grow and learn in a diverse, collaborative culture. We foster relationships and learning through connected global and local teams, promote flexible and diverse career paths and support the development of your knowledge and skills. 

You will be paid a competitive salary, learn from talented individuals across multiple disciplines and be able to thrive in a safe workplace within a collaborative culture. Ignite your career at a place where your distinct potential can find its home.

We respect and value all 

Orica promotes and fosters a culture of inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment everywhere we operate. We treat our people and applicants with fairness, dignity, and respect, getting the best of everyone’s contributions.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, nationality, age, military or veteran, marital or disability status.

Manager, Governance and Reporting

Salary range: $149,739 - $173,174 + superannuation and leave loading
Duration: Permanent full-time role, ongoing
Location: Hybrid with an in-office requirement on Tuesdays and Thursdays in Sydney, McKell. This may be subject to change in line with DCS workplace policies

About 

Join a community-focused organisation that’s using data to drive better decisions and deliver more effective services. It’s a place where people care about the “why” behind the numbers, and where data is used to understand people and improve outcomes, not just to report on them.

You’ll be part of a close-knit team that values collaboration, curiosity, and practical problem solving. They’re connecting systems, improving data quality, and turning insights into actions that genuinely help the business and community.

 

The Opportunity

You’ll join a small, collaborative analytics function reporting into the Manager of Applications. The team is responsible for building a single-client view, improving data quality across multiple systems, and turning operational data into insight that program teams and leaders can act on.

This role suits someone with hands-on experience working with data, someone who has tackled challenging datasets, uncovered insights from imperfect sources, and can work closely with service teams to communicate findings clearly and effectively.

 

What You'll Do

In this role, you’ll translate complex data into actionable insights, support decision-making across the organisation, and work closely with teams to ensure information is accurate, meaningful, and accessible. Your work will combine hands-on analysis with stakeholder collaboration to make data genuinely useful.

Design, build and maintain dashboards and visualisations (Power BI).
Write and optimise SQL for reporting, extraction and transformation.
Pull together data from multiple enterprise systems (Dynamics 365, CRM, HRIS, Payroll, etc.) to support a single client view.
Investigate and improve data quality, validation, cleansing and documentation.
Translate business questions into clear metrics, insights and simple stories for non-technical teams.

Ad Text:
Did you know?

The mission of the Australian Federal Police is to provide dynamic and effective law enforcement to the people of Australia. It provides policing throughout Australia in relation to the prevention and detection of crimes against the Commonwealth, its laws and integrity, and community police services to the Community of the ACT.

Working for the AFP provides you with a diverse and rewarding career. Whether you immerse yourself in a position on the frontline, or provide critical operational or professional support, the work you do makes a big impact on the people of Australia.

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Six weeks (30 days) of paid annual leave per year + additional paid Christmas stand down
4 extra days of mandatory rest leave per year
18 days of paid personal leave per year
Generous superannuation of 15.4%
18 weeks paid Parental Leave for Primary Caregiver (and 14 weeks for Secondary Caregiver) + additional unpaid leave entitlements for up until 24 months from the child’s date of birth or placement
Generous salaries and incremental salary progression governed by the AFP Enterprise Agreement
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements that support work-life balance
Health & wellbeing services – with a focus on early intervention, education and prevention
Access to ongoing training and professional development opportunities
What is the role?

The Quintiq Technical Specialist role is attached to Business Services, CFO Command and reports to the Quintiq Coordinator.

The role provides third tier technical support, as well as working closely with AFP business areas, including Payroll Services and SAP, to ensure business continuity required to support time recording, rostering, workforce planning, and pay affecting Enterprise Agreement (EA) conditions.

The role will maintain the AFP’s Quintiq time recording and rostering solution and is responsible for the daily system monitoring and administration, API interpretation, data connectivity, and system integration including maintaining system continuity.

The role will also require solution architecture expertise, advanced data modelling capability, and specialist knowledge of Quintiq's backend solution that underpins rostering, workforce management, and pay affecting calculations across the AFP.

It requires the capability to build forecasting models, conduct workforce analytics, design decision to analyse roster efficiency using statistical data, database structures, error handling frameworks, complex test scenarios using various techniques.

The Technical Specialist contributes to the achievement of the organisation’s strategic and operational outcomes in accordance with the regulatory framework, AFP values, and the AFP Governance Framework.

What will you do?
Monitor system performance to proactively identify and resolve potential issues.
Develop automated data quality checks to identify duplicates, missing values or anomalies.
Monitor progress and adjust strategies to ensure sustained improvement.
Validating Quintiq complies with the Enterprise Agreement and Industrial Instrument regulations.
Provide training and development to build capability and adaptability.
Collaborate with vendors and stakeholders to align integration solutions with business needs.
Track and report on data accuracy metrics to drive accountability and improvement.
Essential Requirements
Negative Vetting 1 (Secret) security clearance or ability to obtain.
Demonstrated functional SAP experience, API solution integration, system middleware and experience in SAP HR/Payroll.
Demonstrated experience in audit assurance compliance to comply with the Enterprise Agreement and Industrial Instrument regulations.
Desirable Requirements
Demonstrated experience to deliver high quality customer service through effective, solution oriented approaches.
Additional Information
This role is at an AFP Technical Specialist Level 1
This role is located in ACT and succesful applicants must be located in ACT or relocate at own expense

Job Title: Senior Technical Analyst – Cloud Modernisation & Legacy Systems
Location: Canberra ACT / Melbourne VIC / Adelaide SA (Hybrid)
Rate: $120-$140 per hour
Contract: 12+12+12 months

About Us
Canberra Consulting focuses on delivering highly skilled and qualified professionals to our clients. We have the experience and capability to understand your specific requirements and match them to the right environment, ensuring professional satisfaction and development. We focus on fit — for both our clients and our candidates — and give you every opportunity to thrive.

The Opportunity
We are seeking several Senior Technical Analysts to join the Cloud Infrastructure, Architecture and Strategy team of a large Federal Government agency undergoing significant digital transformation. This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of legacy systems analysis and forward-looking cloud strategy — you will be the critical link between strategic solution architects and the day-to-day operational reality of a complex ICT environment.
Working hybrid across Canberra, Melbourne or Adelaide, you will analyse legacy application estates, validate technical requirements for costing requests, map data flows and integration points, and help define the path to a modern, sovereign cloud environment. You will also prepare briefing material for executive boards and governance committees, making this a highly visible and influential position.
This role is expected to commence 1 May 2026 on an initial 12-month contract, with up to two further 12-month extensions available.

Key Responsibilities

Conduct deep-dive analysis of legacy application estates to identify dependencies, integration points, and technical debt
Support the development of detailed Technical Roadmap components, mapping application lifecycles and identifying optimal decommissioning points
Document current-state technical architectures to inform cloud migration strategies
Act as the technical triage point for External Costing Requests (ECRs) — validating scope accuracy and feasibility prior to submission
Liaise with solution and technical architecture teams to assess proposed designs against digital strategy and investment frameworks
Map data flows and interface specifications; define functional and non-functional requirements for engineering teams
Translate high-level architectural designs into detailed specifications for delivery teams
Engage across internal business teams, shared service providers (including Services Australia, Defence, Finance and Health) and external vendors
Develop technical assessments to support major capability decisions including cloud enablement and EDW migrations
Prepare briefing materials for Executive Boards and internal Governance Committees
Contribute to options analysis and business/process dependency mapping to support the architecture stream
Skills and Experience
Technical Capabilities

Strong technical literacy — ability to read and interpret API specifications, database schemas (SQL), infrastructure diagrams, and legacy mainframe/mid-range system documentation
Demonstrated analytical rigour in breaking down complex technical problems into clear documented outputs (Functional Specifications, Interface Control Documents, etc.)
Data fluency — experience analysing complex data structures and mapping migrations
Proficiency with modelling and work management tools such as Visio, Jira, Tableau and/or PowerBI
Experience working within or alongside shared services models; familiarity with ECR/costing processes is highly regarded
Some exposure to UX/DX design and usability evaluation is advantageous
Behavioural Capabilities

 About the role:

This is a full‑time 12 Month FTC, based in our North Ryde office for a Senior Business Analyst supporting transformation initiatives, rather than BAU operational activity. The role delivers high‑quality business analysis across complex, project‑based transformation programs, working closely with Project Managers, Change Managers, and transformation delivery teams.

You will work closely with the business to translate customer insights and strategic direction into well‑defined initiatives, shaping work through the lifecycle from definition and analysis through to implementation. The role focuses on understanding current challenges, defining future‑state processes, and ensuring solutions deliver measurable business outcomes.

 

Key responsibilities:

Lead end‑to‑end business analysis activities to define, validate, and optimise business requirements across transformation initiatives
Analyse business functions and processes to inform operational objectives and strategic decision‑making
Develop clear current‑state and future‑state process maps, workflows, and specifications to support solution design and investment cases
Lead the design of "To Be" business processes, ensuring alignment with organisational standards and transformation objectives
Engage and influence senior business and technical stakeholders to ensure solutions meet requirements and deliver intended benefits
Provide strategic insight into business drivers, risks, benefits, and benefits realisation opportunities
Conduct data analysis to identify trends, improvement opportunities, and measurable outcomes
Oversee test planning, acceptance criteria, and UAT to ensure solution readiness and high‑quality delivery

 

What you bring to the role:

5+ years' experience in Business Analysis roles across relevant industries.
Strong business analysis capability, including requirements elicitation, process modelling, and solution evaluation.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to engage business users, senior leaders, and technical stakeholders.
Proven facilitation, negotiation, and influencing skills across complex stakeholder groups.
Solid understanding of SDLC and delivery methodologies, including Agile and Waterfall.
Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, with the ability to translate business needs into clear requirements and process designs.
Experience leading analysis on medium to large or complex initiatives.
Proven experience supporting end‑to‑end delivery, including requirements, testing, and transition into BAU.

Job Title: Principal Reservoir Engineer & Decision Risk Analyst
Ad Text:
Position Title: Principal Reservoir Engineer and Decision Risk Analyst

Perth Based
2 Year Max Term Employee
Supporting the Subsurface Gas Supply Forecasting Team

At INPEX, our energy is helping to shape a brighter future.

We are proudly supporting positive outcomes in Australian communities, through jobs, training, and social and economic benefits. Our Australian energy portfolio includes Ichthys LNG which we operate, plus participating interests in Prelude FLNG, Darwin LNG, Van Gogh and Ravensworth. We are actively pursuing carbon, capture, and storage opportunities to help provide a cleaner upstream business for Ichthys LNG.

Our inclusive culture is key to our success – valuing diversity and bringing us together as a connected and innovative team. As an international company, INPEX offers unique opportunities to collaborate with colleagues from across the globe. We are keen to attract and develop passionate, motivated, and safety-minded people from all backgrounds who want to help support society’s growing need for secure, affordable, and clean energy.

We are seeking a Principal Reservoir Engineer and Decision Risk Analyst to join our INPEX Subsurface team in Perth on an Employee 2 Year Max Term contract. Join us in helping shape a brighter energy future.

Key responsibilities:

Significant experience and competency in reservoir engineering, reservoir simulation, and integrated production modelling
Generate probabilistic integrated production modelling forecasts
Expand models to integrate new fields and wells in the forecasts
Perform and document thorough QC of inputs and model results
Assess value and trade-off of multiple depletion strategies, development scenarios, development sequences
Forecast reliability review: perform lookbacks on forecast and drive continuous improvement
Prepare technical documents and reports supporting recommendations, business plan forecast, assumptions, constraints, and gas supply strategy
Generate case maps of scenarios to assess
Significant experience in economic analysis and decision analysis
Generate economic metrics from production forecasts
Develop probabilistic decision trees to recommend optimal decision under uncertainties and constraints

What we’re looking for:

We welcome candidates from diverse backgrounds and experiences. You will be a strong fit for this role if you bring: 

Minimum of degree qualifications in Petroleum Geoscience or Engineering
Post-graduate petroleum qualification preferred
Demonstratable experience working in the oil and gas industry on exploration and development projects.
Experience working in a multi-disciplinary subsurface team
Previous experience working on the North West Australian shelf is preferable
Experience of using Petrel, tNavigator, IPM suite, Quorum software

This role is for applicants residing in or willing to relocate to Perth.

Closing date for applications is 22.04.2026. INPEX reserves the right to close applications early should a suitable pool of candidates be identified. Please note that direct feedback on applications will only be provided to interviewed candidates.

What We Offer: 

Leave & Flexibility: 

16 weeks of fully paid parental leave.
Options to purchase additional leave.
Dedication to flexible work arrangements, encompassing variable hours
INPEX Foundation Day, discretionary leave, and community volunteering leave.

Remuneration & Financial Benefits:

Competitive remuneration package with performance‑based incentives.
Superannuation above the standard guarantee.
Salary packaging options, including novated leasing.
Reward and recognition programs, including access to discounts from over 350 retailers.

Career Growth & Development:  

Clear pathways for career development and internal progression.
Structured leadership development programs with ongoing support.
Dedicated study leave and access to learning and development opportunities.

Wellbeing:

Access to wellbeing initiatives, including gym membership options and health resources.
A supportive workplace focused on safety, balance and sustainable work practices.

Additional benefits such as Japanese language classes are available at select locations, including Perth.

We value diversity in our workplace – including beliefs, cultures, and lifestyles, so we can bring our true selves to work every day. If you believe that you have most but not all the requirements outlined above, we encourage you to still apply.

We welcome all candidates to advise us throughout the recruitment process if we can support you with accommodations to make this a more inclusive experience.

As Chief Digital Officer, you will provide strategic advice and direction to the development and improvement of EDQ's business information and supporting information technology systems.

You will:
• Strategically lead ICT planning and integration across the organisation and manage the delivery of digital business platforms to improve service delivery and customer experience.
• Cultivate strong business partnerships and key stakeholder relationships across EDQ and with external providers.
• Provide expert advice on EDQ's data and system structures guiding digital planning and design, and investment decision-making
• Lead the development and implementation of enterprise architecture approach and design, including organisation-wide ‘blueprints', design artefacts, guidelines, and investment strategies
• Work closely with critical stakeholders, both internal and external to EDQ to advance the digital agenda and translate business needs into clear technical requirements for delivery by vendors and partners
• Ensure that ICT Service Level Agreements (SLA) and contracts are in place with service partners and external organisations and that they are managed effectively whilst constantly ensuring that value is being received through these arrangements.
• Provide oversight and provision of expert advice on the delivery of ICT projects within/or impacting on EDQ and ensure that these are aligned with the Queensland Government Enterprise Architecture (QGEA) portfolio, program and project management and assurance policy.
• Analyse and provide strategic advice to the Executive Director and the Executive Leadership Team with respect to the delivery of digital opportunities, business improvements and systems.
